rationale: The citing paper is a retailer-level soda-tax DID study on pricing/promotions and conduct effectiveness, while the cited paper is a household-level liquidity-constraints study on bulk buying and sale-timing; the overlap is only indirect (consumer response to sales/promotions). The matched evidence is sparse (just one DOI match, no reference text or in-text citation context), so there is no clear sign of substantive methodological or empirical dependence. Given this limited evidence, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only, but not strong enough to clearly keep.
